findings.suggestedFix: Pick one convention for the whole list (recommend pure UAQ: Fitr 2027 = Mar 9, Adha 2027 = May 16, Fitr 2028 = Feb 26), and rewrite the comment to honestly state the convention used. If the 30-day-Ramadan convention is kept for near years, apply it uniformly (2029 would become Feb 15, 2031 would become Jan 25) and document it.
findings.correction: Pick one convention and apply it consistently, then fix the comment. Option A (pure UAQ, matches the comment's claim): change Fitr 2027 to Mar 9, Adha 2027 to May 16, Fitr 2028 to Feb 26 in eidGuide.ts, and update islamicCalendar.ts accordingly (Ramadan 1448 ends Mar 8, Fitr 1448 = Mar 9, 1 Dhul Hijjah 1448 = May 7, Arafah = May 15, Adha = May 16; Ramadan 1449/Fitr 1449 shift -1 similarly). Option B (keep current 2027/2028 dates): rewrite the comment to state that near-term Fitr entries assume a 30-day Ramadan (+1 day vs UAQ) and that Adha 2027 differs from UAQ by one day, and either pad 2029/2031 Fitr the same way or explicitly note the convention switch. In either case delete the false 'agree on all of them' sentence.

findings.title: Qurbani time window is self-contradictory: 'until sunset of 13th Dhul Hijjah (3 days, 2 nights)'
findings.description: Masala adha-qr-2 says qurbani 'continues until sunset of 13th Dhul Hijjah (3 days, 2 nights)'. These two statements describe different rulings: 10th-13th is 4 days / 3 nights (the Shafi'i and one Hanbali-reported view), while '3 days, 2 nights' is the Hanafi/Maliki/mainstream-Hanbali view, which ends at sunset of the 12th Dhul Hijjah, not the 13th. As written, the text is internally contradictory, and a Hanafi user (the app's most detailed madhhab elsewhere — the Urdu text repeats the same contradiction) who sacrifices on the 13th would have an invalid qurbani by their own school. Note adha-ts-1 (line 631) also says 'Qurbani remains valid on all three days' of tashreeq (11-13), which silently adopts the 4-day view without a madhhab note, unlike the careful madhabNote treatment given elsewhere in the same file.
findings.evidence: App: 'Qurbani begins after the Eid prayer on 10th Dhul Hijjah and continues until sunset of 13th Dhul Hijjah (3 days, 2 nights).' Authoritative position: Hanafi/Maliki/Hanbali — udhiyah days are the 10th, 11th and 12th (ends at sunset of the 12th); Shafi'i — extends until sunset of the 13th (4 days). 10th-to-13th can never equal '3 days, 2 nights'.
findings.sources: https://islamqa.info/en/answers/36651 (time of udhiyah: three days per the majority, four days per Shafi'i view); Al-Hidayah (Hanafi) and Al-Mughni (Ibn Qudamah) as cited by the app itself for adjacent masail
findings.suggestedFix: Either: 'until sunset of 12th Dhul Hijjah (3 days, 2 nights); the Shafi'i school extends it to sunset of the 13th', using the existing madhabNote field — and fix the Urdu text ('۱۳ ذوالحجہ کے غروب تک (۳ دن، ۲ راتیں)') the same way; or keep 'until the 13th' and change the parenthetical to '(4 days)' with a Hanafi madhabNote that their deadline is sunset of the 12th.
findings.correction: Pick one consistent ruling and flag the other as a madhhab difference. Recommended (matches the app's Hanafi lean and the '3 days, 2 nights' phrasing already present): line 541 → 'Qurbani begins after the Eid prayer on 10th Dhul Hijjah and continues until sunset of 12th Dhul Hijjah (3 days, 2 nights). The best time is the 10th, immediately after the Eid prayer.' Mirror the fix in the Urdu (line 543: ۱۲ ذوالحجہ کے غروب تک). Add a madhabNote: 'According to the Shafi\'i school, qurbani remains valid until sunset of the 13th Dhul Hijjah (4 days).' Also fix adha-ts-1 (line 631): change 'Qurbani remains valid on all three days' to note that per the Hanafi/Maliki/Hanbali view qurbani ends at sunset of the 12th, while the Shafi'i view allows it on the 13th — or add a matching madhabNote there.

findings.title: Six-month minimum-age concession wrongly extended to goats and mislabeled as Hanafi-specific; contradicts qurbani.ts's own 12-month minimum
findings.description: Masala adha-qr-3 says: 'Goat / Sheep — 1 person. Min. 1 full year. (Hanafi: 6+ months if large & healthy)'. Two errors: (1) the six-month concession (the jadha'ah) applies ONLY to sheep, never to goats — in all four madhhabs, including the Hanafi school, a goat must have completed one full year; a six-month goat is invalid qurbani in every school. Attaching the parenthetical to the combined 'Goat / Sheep' line invites users to buy a 6-9-month goat, invalidating their sacrifice. (2) The sheep concession is not uniquely Hanafi — it is accepted across the madhhabs based on the hadith of Muslim 1963 ('Sacrifice only a musinnah, unless it is difficult for you, in which case slaughter a jadha'ah of sheep'). The Urdu text has the same flaw: 'بکرا/بکری/دنبہ — ۱ سال (حنفی: ۶ ماہ اگر بڑا ہو)' lumps goat (بکرا/بکری) with sheep. Internal inconsistency: qurbani.ts (the calculator users actually interact with) sets minAgeMonths: 12 for both goat and sheep with no 6-month note (lines 28 and 40), so the two surfaces disagree.
findings.evidence: App: 'Goat / Sheep — 1 person. Min. 1 full year. (Hanafi: 6+ months if large & healthy)'. Authoritative: the jadha' concession is specific to sheep (da'n); goats (ma'z) must be thaniyy — one full year — per all four schools, including Hanafi fiqh (Al-Hidayah / Radd al-Muhtar). Muslim 1963: '...slaughter a jadha'ah of SHEEP'. Cross-file: /Users/malikabdul/risepath/src/constants/qurbani.ts has minAgeMonths: 12 for goat and for sheep.
findings.sources: https://islamqa.info/en/answers/41899 (age of udhiyah animal: sheep six months/jadha', goat one full year); Sahih Muslim 1963 (https://sunnah.com/muslim:1963); Al-Hidayah (Marghinani), cited by the app itself in adha-qr-1
findings.suggestedFix: Reword to: 'Goat — 1 person. Min. 1 full year. / Sheep — 1 person. Min. 1 full year; a sheep of 6+ months that looks like a yearling is also valid (all schools, per Muslim 1963).' Fix the Urdu equivalently (the ۶-month note must attach only to دنبہ/بھیڑ, not بکرا/بکری). Optionally surface the sheep exception in qurbani.ts so the calculator and guide agree.
findings.correction: In eidGuide.ts adha-qr-3, split the line: '• Goat — 1 person. Min. 1 full year.\n• Sheep — 1 person. Min. 1 full year (a 6-month sheep is valid if so large and healthy it looks a year old — Muslim 1963).' Urdu: '• بکرا/بکری — ۱ شخص، کم از کم ۱ سال\n• دنبہ/بھیڑ — ۱ شخص، ۱ سال (۶ ماہ کا دنبہ جائز اگر اتنا بڑا ہو کہ سال بھر کا لگے)'. Optionally mirror the sheep exception as a note in qurbani.ts so the calculator and guide agree. The concession must never be attached to goats.

findings.title: Zakat ul-Fitr household rule is stated in reverse (Hanafi vs other schools)
findings.description: Masala 'fitr-zf-1' says the Hanafi school has the head of household pay zakat ul-fitr on behalf of all dependents including the wife, and that 'other schools require each adult to pay for themselves.' This is exactly backwards. In the Hanafi school, zakat al-fitr is an individual obligation: a man pays for himself and his minor children but is NOT obliged to pay for his wife (she pays her own if she owns nisab). It is the Shafi'i/Maliki/Hanbali schools that oblige the head of household to pay for everyone whose maintenance he is responsible for, including his wife. A Hanafi wife relying on this text could leave her own wajib unpaid. The Urdu description (line 47) repeats the same reversed claim.
findings.evidence: App: 'In the Hanafi school, the head of the household pays on behalf of all dependents (wife, minor children). Other schools require each adult to pay for themselves.' Authoritative sources: Hanafi — 'it is wajib for a capable person to pay for himself, his minor children... the wife's zakat is not wajib upon the husband'; Shafi'i — wajib to pay 'for oneself as well as those whose maintenance is wajib upon one, such as wife, father and son' (al-Islam.org Five Schools comparison; IslamQA confirms the majority position that the husband pays fitrah for the wife).
findings.suggestedFix: Swap the two positions: 'In the Hanafi school each adult who owns nisab pays for themselves (a father also pays for his minor children); in the Shafi'i, Maliki, and Hanbali schools the head of the household pays on behalf of everyone he is obliged to maintain, including his wife.' Fix the Urdu description to match.
findings.correction: Replace line 45 with: 'Zakat ul-Fitr is obligatory (Wajib) on every free Muslim who possesses food beyond their own and their family's needs on the day and night of Eid. In the Hanafi school it is an individual obligation: each adult (including the wife, from her own wealth) pays for themselves, and the father pays for his minor children. In the Shafi\'i, Maliki, and Hanbali schools, the head of the household pays on behalf of everyone he is obliged to maintain, including his wife.' Update the Urdu on line 47 to match, e.g.: 'حنفی مسلک میں ہر بالغ اپنی طرف سے خود ادا کرتا ہے (بیوی اپنے مال سے) اور والد اپنے نابالغ بچوں کی طرف سے ادا کرتا ہے۔ دیگر مسالک میں گھر کا سرپرست بیوی سمیت تمام زیرِ کفالت افراد کی طرف سے ادا کرتا ہے۔'

findings.title: Qurbani household rule is stated in reverse (Hanafi per-person vs majority one-per-household)
findings.description: Masala 'adha-qr-1' says: 'Hanafi: Wajib. One qurbani per household; the head of family acts on behalf of dependents. Shafi'i / Maliki / Hanbali: Sunnah Mu'akkadah — one per individual adult who can afford it.' The household attribution is reversed. In the Hanafi school qurbani is wajib on EACH individual who owns nisab — there is no household pooling; a wife who owns nisab must offer her own qurbani. It is the Shafi'i/Maliki/Hanbali (majority) view that one sheep from the head of household suffices the whole family (hadith of Abu Ayyub al-Ansari). Masala 'adha-qr-1b' (line 530) repeats the error, calling household pooling 'the Hanafi household-pooling view' and saying 'other schools require each adult to do their own.' Both Urdu descriptions (lines 521, 532) repeat it. A Hanafi family relying on this would leave wajib qurbanis unfulfilled.
findings.evidence: App: 'Hanafi: Wajib. One qurbani per household...' / 'in the Hanafi household-pooling view (other schools require each adult to do their own when capable)'. Authoritative sources: 'According to the Hanafi madhab, both the husband and wife must donate a minimum of one Qurbani each if they... possess wealth more than their needs'; 'According to the majority view (Shafi'i, Maliki, and Hanbali), one Qurbani from the head of the household is sufficient to cover the entire family' (hadith of Abu Ayyub: a man would sacrifice one sheep on behalf of himself and his household).
findings.suggestedFix: In adha-qr-1 and adha-qr-1b (and both Urdu texts): Hanafi = wajib on every sane adult who owns nisab, each such person (including a wife with her own wealth) offers their own; Shafi'i/Maliki/Hanbali = sunnah mu'akkadah and one sheep suffices the entire household. Also update the adha-qr-1b exemption bullet for 'a wife / adult child' to attribute pooling to the majority schools, not Hanafi.
findings.correction: In eidGuide.ts swap the attributions in all four places. adha-qr-1 (line 519) should read approximately: "Hanafi: **Wajib** (obligatory) on each adult who owns nisab — including a wife with her own wealth; one person's qurbani does not cover other nisab-owning family members (though a husband may arrange his wife's qurbani with her permission). Shafi'i / Maliki / Hanbali: **Sunnah Mu'akkadah** — one sheep offered by the head of the household suffices on behalf of the whole family (hadith of Abu Ayyub al-Ansari, Tirmidhi 1505)." adha-qr-1b's fifth bullet (line 530) should say a wife/adult child is covered by the head-of-household's single qurbani in the Shafi'i/Maliki/Hanbali view, whereas in the Hanafi school each nisab-owning adult must have their own. Mirror the same swap in the Urdu descriptions at lines 521 and 532. Optionally add Tirmidhi 1505 to the source line.

findings.title: Eid prayer takbir counts misattributed: Maliki and Hanbali use 6 extra takbirs, not 7
findings.description: Masala 'fitr-sal-2' groups 'Shafi'i / Maliki / Hanbali: 7 takbeers in the first rakat after the opening (before Fatiha); 5 takbeers in the second rakat.' Only the Shafi'i school has 7 additional takbirs after takbirat al-ihram (7+5 = 12 extra). The Maliki and Hanbali schools have 6 additional takbirs after the opening takbir in the first rakah and 5 in the second (11 extra). The Hanafi 3+3 description in the same masala is correct. The Urdu text (line 208) repeats the 7-for-all-three claim.
findings.evidence: App: 'Shafi'i / Maliki / Hanbali: 7 takbeers in the first rakat after the opening.' Authoritative source (Darul Fiqh, citing Mughni al-Muhtaj and Mawahib al-Jalil): Shafi'i — 'an additional 12 takbeer; 7... in the first rakaat'; Maliki — 'an additional 11 takbeer; 6 takbeer... after the takbeer tahreema'; Hanbali — 'in conformity with the Maliki school... 11 takbeer; 6 takbeer when commencing.'
findings.suggestedFix: Split the grouping: 'Shafi'i: 7 extra takbirs in the first rakat, 5 in the second. Maliki / Hanbali: 6 extra in the first rakat, 5 in the second.' Update Urdu accordingly.
findings.correction: In src/constants/eidGuide.ts (masala fitr-sal-2, lines 206 and 208), split the grouped attribution: "Shafi'i: 7 takbeers in the first rakat after the opening (before Fatiha); 5 in the second. Maliki / Hanbali: 6 takbeers after the opening takbeer in the first rakat (7 counting the opening); 5 in the second rakat." Apply the same split in the Urdu description (line 208): Shafi'i — sat (7) takbirein; Maliki/Hanbali — chhe (6) za'id takbirein after takbeer-e-tahreema.

findings.title: Qurbani end time self-contradictory: 'until sunset of 13th' labeled '(3 days, 2 nights)', and 13th-day validity is the minority Shafi'i view
findings.description: Masala 'adha-qr-2' says qurbani 'continues until sunset of 13th Dhul Hijjah (3 days, 2 nights)'. These two statements contradict each other: 10th-to-13th-sunset is 4 days/3 nights; '3 days, 2 nights' describes the Hanafi (and Maliki/Hanbali) period that ends at sunset of the 12th. The 13th-day extension is the Shafi'i position only, yet it is presented as universal in an app whose guide otherwise foregrounds the Hanafi ruling (and whose Urdu text repeats '۱۳ ذوالحجہ کے غروب تک (۳ دن، ۲ راتیں)'). A Hanafi user slaughtering on the 13th based on this would have an invalid qurbani by their school.
findings.evidence: App: 'continues until sunset of 13th Dhul Hijjah (3 days, 2 nights)'. Authoritative sources: Hanafi (and the majority of fuqaha) — qurbani days are the 10th, 11th and 12th, ending at sunset on the 12th ('The Fuqaha are unanimous that slaughtering on the 13th of Dhul Hijjah is not permissible, with the exception of Imam Shafi'i' — Hadith Answers); Shafi'i — four days through sunset of the 13th.
findings.suggestedFix: State both positions explicitly: 'Hanafi / Maliki / Hanbali: from after Eid prayer on the 10th until sunset of the 12th (3 days, 2 nights). Shafi'i: until sunset of the 13th (4 days).' Fix English and Urdu.
findings.correction: Make the masala internally consistent and madhhab-aware. Recommended (matches the guide's Hanafi framing): 'Qurbani begins after the Eid prayer on 10th Dhul Hijjah and continues until sunset of 12th Dhul Hijjah (3 days, 2 nights). According to the Shafi'i school it extends until sunset of the 13th. The best time is the 10th, immediately after the Eid prayer.' Mirror the same fix in the Urdu (descriptionUr): change ۱۳ to ۱۲ for the main ruling and optionally add a Shafi'i note. Alternatively, if keeping the 13th as the end date, the parenthetical must become '(4 days)' and be labeled as the Shafi'i position.

findings.title: 'Qurbani remains valid on all three days' of Tashreeq (11th-13th) presented as universal fact
findings.description: Masala 'adha-ts-1' states that during Ayyam al-Tashreeq (11, 12, 13 Dhul Hijjah) 'Qurbani remains valid on all three days.' Validity on the 13th is the Shafi'i position only; Hanafi, Maliki, and Hanbali hold qurbani ends at sunset of the 12th. Stated as universal fact, this compounds the error in adha-qr-2 and could lead Hanafi users (the app's primary fiqh framing) to an invalid sacrifice on the 13th. Urdu text (line 633) repeats it.
findings.evidence: App: 'Qurbani remains valid on all three days. Takbeer al-Tashreeq continues... through Asr of the 13th.' Authoritative source: 'The Fuqaha are unanimous that slaughtering on the 13th of Dhul Hijjah is not permissible, with the exception of Imam Shafi'i, who has permitted this' (Hadith Answers). The takbeer-through-Asr-of-13th part is correct; the qurbani-validity part is not universal.
findings.suggestedFix: Change to: 'Qurbani remains valid on the 11th and 12th (and on the 13th in the Shafi'i school).'
findings.correction: In adha-ts-1 (line 631) replace "Qurbani remains valid on all three days." with a madhhab-qualified statement, e.g.: "Hanafi / Maliki / Hanbali: qurbani is valid only on the first two Tashreeq days, ending at sunset of the 12th. Shafi'i: valid through sunset of the 13th." Mirror in Urdu (line 633). Also fix adha-qr-2 (line 541): "continues until sunset of 12th Dhul Hijjah (3 days, 2 nights) in the Hanafi school; the Shafi'i school allows until sunset of the 13th." Keep the takbeer-through-Asr-of-13th text unchanged (it is correct).

findings.title: Six-month age concession wrongly extended to goats — it applies only to sheep
findings.description: Masala 'adha-qr-3' lists 'Goat / Sheep — 1 person. Min. 1 full year. (Hanafi: 6+ months if large & healthy)' — attaching the six-month concession to both goat and sheep. By consensus of all four schools, the jadha'a (6-month) concession applies ONLY to sheep (hadith: 'Do not slaughter anything but a musinnah, unless it is difficult for you, in which case slaughter a jadha'ah of sheep' — Muslim 1963). A goat must be a full year old in every school, including Hanafi. The Urdu text (line 554: 'بکرا/بکری/دنبہ — ... (حنفی: ۶ ماہ اگر بڑا ہو)') has the same error. A user sacrificing a 7-month goat on this advice would have an invalid qurbani. Note: qurbani.ts correctly sets goat minAgeMonths: 12, so the two screens currently disagree.
findings.evidence: App: 'Goat / Sheep — ... Min. 1 full year. (Hanafi: 6+ months if large & healthy)'. Authoritative sources: 'a jadha'ah of goats (young goat) is not sufficient as a sacrifice'; 'A jadha'ah of sheep is a sheep that has reached the age of six months, according to the Hanafis and Hanbalis' — the Hanafi large-looking-as-one-year rule is specific to sheep (IslamQA 41899; Darul Uloom TT Hanafi fatwa; Sahih Muslim 1963).
findings.suggestedFix: Split the line: 'Goat — 1 person. Min. 1 full year (all schools). Sheep — 1 person. Min. 1 full year, or 6+ months if so well-grown it looks a year old.' Fix the Urdu line to attach the 6-month rule to دنبہ/بھیڑ only.
findings.correction: Split the bullet so the concession is sheep-only. English (line 552): '• Goat — 1 person. Min. 1 full year.\n• Sheep — 1 person. Min. 1 full year. (Hanafi: 6+ months acceptable for sheep only, if large and healthy enough to look a year old)'. Urdu (line 554): '• بکرا/بکری — ۱ شخص، کم از کم ۱ سال\n• دنبہ/بھیڑ — ۱ شخص، کم از کم ۱ سال (حنفی: صرف دنبہ/بھیڑ ۶ ماہ کا جائز اگر بڑا اور صحت مند ہو)'. Optionally add Muslim 1963 to the source field (line 555), since that hadith is the basis for the age rule.

findings.title: Three Eid dates are one day later than Umm al-Qura, contradicting the file's own verification claim
findings.description: The comment above UPCOMING_EID_DATES claims the dates are 'anchored to Saudi/Umm al-Qura' and 'verified against the official Umm al-Qura tables (Aladhan hToG calendarMethod=UAQ...)'. I re-ran that exact check: three entries are +1 day off UAQ — Eid ul-Fitr 2027 (app: Mar 10; UAQ 1 Shawwal 1448 = Mar 9, 2027), Eid ul-Adha 2027 (app: May 17; UAQ 10 Dhul Hijjah 1448 = May 16, 2027), and Eid ul-Fitr 2028 (app: Feb 27; UAQ 1 Shawwal 1449 = Feb 26, 2028). All eight other entries (Adha 2026 = May 27, Adha 2028 = May 5, Fitr/Adha 2029-2031) match UAQ exactly, so the table mixes conventions internally. Both the Aladhan UAQ API and the independent al-habib.info Umm al-Qura tables agree on the three corrections.
findings.evidence: App lines 675-677: new Date(2027, 2, 10) / new Date(2027, 4, 17) / new Date(2028, 1, 27). Umm al-Qura: api.aladhan.com/v1/hToG/01-10-1448?calendarMethod=UAQ -> 09-03-2027; /10-12-1448 -> 16-05-2027; /01-10-1449 -> 26-02-2028. al-habib.info UAQ calendar confirms: Eid al-Fitr 1448 = Tue Mar 9 2027, Eid al-Adha 1448 = Sun May 16 2027, Eid al-Fitr 1449 = Sat Feb 26 2028.
findings.suggestedFix: Change line 675 to new Date(2027, 2, 9), line 676 to new Date(2027, 4, 16), line 677 to new Date(2028, 1, 26) — or, if the +1 offset is an intentional South-Asia convention inherited from islamicCalendar.ts, apply it consistently to all entries and correct the comment that claims UAQ agreement.
findings.correction: In /Users/malikabdul/risepath/src/constants/eidGuide.ts: change line 675 to new Date(2027, 2, 9) (Eid ul-Fitr 2027 = Mar 9), line 676 to new Date(2027, 4, 16) (Eid ul-Adha 2027 = May 16), line 677 to new Date(2028, 1, 26) (Eid ul-Fitr 2028 = Feb 26). The same +1 errors propagate through /Users/malikabdul/risepath/src/constants/islamicCalendar.ts and its derived entries, which must be fixed together: Ramadan 1448 = Feb 8 – Mar 8, 2027 (29 days), fitr-1448 baseDate = Mar 9; Laylat al-Qadr 1448 nights shift back one day accordingly (odd nights 21/23/25/27/29 = Feb 27, Mar 1, 3, 5, 7 evening per the file's existing night convention); 1 Dhul Hijjah 1448 = May 7, Arafah = May 15, adha-1448 = May 16, 2027; Ramadan 1449 = Jan 28 – Feb 25, 2028 (29 days), fitr-1449 = Feb 26, with 1449 Qadr nights shifted back one day as well. All per Umm al-Qura (Aladhan UAQ API, al-habib.info tables, and KACST hijridate all agree).

findings.title: Bukhari 5547 cited for the three-way meat division, but that hadith is about distributing animals among the Companions
findings.description: Masala 'adha-qr-6' (Distribution of Meat — thirds for self/relatives/poor) cites 'Ibn Qudama, Bukhari 5547'. Sahih al-Bukhari 5547 is the hadith of Uqbah ibn Amir in which the Prophet distributed sacrificial ANIMALS among his Companions and Uqbah received a jadha'a — it says nothing about dividing the meat into thirds. The three-way division is a fiqh recommendation (the Ibn Qudama citation is appropriate; the usual hadith basis for eating/feeding/storing is e.g. Bukhari 5569 / Muslim 1971 'eat, give in charity and store'). The ruling text itself, including 'mustahabb, not obligatory', is correct.
findings.evidence: App source field: 'Ibn Qudama, Bukhari 5547'. sunnah.com/bukhari:5547: the Prophet 'distributed sacrificial animals among his companions... Uqba received a young goat (Jadha'a)... "Slaughter it as a sacrifice"' — chapter 'The distribution of the animals among the people', not meat distribution.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace the citation with 'Ibn Qudama (al-Mughni); cf. Bukhari 5569 / Muslim 1971 (eat, give in charity and store)' or drop the Bukhari number.
findings.correction: In /Users/malikabdul/risepath/src/constants/eidGuide.ts line 608, change source from 'Ibn Qudama, Bukhari 5547' to something like 'Ibn Qudama (al-Mughni); cf. Bukhari 5569, Muslim 1971' — Bukhari 5569 / Muslim 1971 ('eat, feed others, and store') is the hadith basis usually paired with the fiqh recommendation of dividing the meat into thirds; Bukhari 5547 is about distributing sacrificial animals among the Companions, not meat distribution.

findings.title: Urdu Tashreeq card always says 'third day' regardless of which Tashreeq day it is
findings.description: The Ayyam al-Tashreeq override builds the Urdu title as `ایامِ تشریق — تیسرا دن ${h.day - 10}/۳`. 'تیسرا دن' means 'third day', so on 11 Dhul Hijjah the card reads 'Ayyam-e-Tashreeq — third day 1/۳' and on the 12th 'third day 2/۳'. The English ('day X of 3') and Arabic ('اليوم X من ٣') variants are correct; only the Urdu hardcodes the ordinal. Also note the interpolated digit renders as a Western numeral against the Urdu '۳'.
findings.evidence: App: titleUr: `ایامِ تشریق — تیسرا دن ${h.day - 10}/۳` vs titleEn: `Ayyam al-Tashreeq — day ${h.day - 10} of 3`. 'تیسرا' is the ordinal 'third', factually wrong on days 11 and 12 of Dhul Hijjah.
findings.sources: Internal consistency check against the English/Arabic strings in the same object (no external source needed for a copy bug).
findings.suggestedFix: Change to `ایامِ تشریق — دن ${h.day - 10}/۳` (or map 1/2/3 to پہلا/دوسرا/تیسرا دن).
findings.correction: Change line 177 to interpolate the day number like the English/Arabic variants, e.g. titleUr: `ایامِ تشریق — دن ${h.day - 10}/۳` or better with Urdu ordinals: day 11 → پہلا دن, 12 → دوسرا دن, 13 → تیسرا دن. Optionally convert the interpolated digit to an Eastern Arabic numeral (۱/۲/۳) to match the hardcoded ۳.
